indemnity.name: Indemnity Clause
indemnity.description: A clause where one party agrees to compensate the other for losses, damages, or liabilities
standardFeatures: Mutual indemnification between parties
Carve-outs for gross negligence and willful misconduct
Cap on indemnification obligations
Prompt notice requirement
Right to control defense
Cooperation obligations
redFlags: Unilateral (one-sided) indemnification
No cap on indemnification liability
Indemnity for indirect or consequential losses
Indemnity for indemnitee's own negligence
No notice requirement before triggering indemnity
Unlimited scope covering all possible claims
No carve-out for pre-existing liabilities

limitationOfLiability.name: Limitation of Liability Clause
limitationOfLiability.description: A clause that caps the amount of damages one party can recover from the other
standardFeatures: Cap equal to fees paid in preceding 12 months
Mutual limitation applying to both parties
Exclusion of indirect, consequential, and punitive damages
Carve-outs for death/personal injury, fraud, and wilful misconduct
Carve-out for IP infringement claims
Carve-out for data breach/confidentiality obligations
redFlags: Asymmetric cap (lower for one party)
No carve-out for fraud or wilful misconduct
Cap lower than total contract value
Exclusion of direct damages
No carve-out for confidentiality breaches
Cap on indemnity obligations lower than indemnity exposure
Unlimited liability for customer but capped for vendor

termination.name: Termination Clause
termination.description: A clause specifying the conditions under which either party may end the contract
standardFeatures: Termination for cause with cure period (typically 30 days)
Termination for convenience with reasonable notice (30-90 days)
Specific grounds for immediate termination (insolvency, material breach)
Post-termination obligations and survival provisions
Wind-down period for transition
Payment obligations upon termination
redFlags: No cure period before termination for cause
Unilateral right to terminate for convenience only for one party
Very short termination notice period (less than 30 days)
No survival provisions for key obligations
Automatic termination on minor breaches
No transition assistance obligation
Termination fee that acts as a penalty

confidentiality.name: Confidentiality / NDA Clause
confidentiality.description: A clause protecting sensitive information shared between parties from disclosure to third parties
standardFeatures: Clear definition of confidential information
Standard exceptions (publicly known, independently developed, legally compelled)
Obligation to maintain confidentiality using reasonable measures
Limited disclosure to employees/advisors on need-to-know basis
Return or destruction of confidential information on termination
Reasonable term (2-5 years post-disclosure)
redFlags: No definition or overly broad definition of confidential information
Perpetual confidentiality obligations
No exception for legally compelled disclosure
Obligation to guarantee confidentiality of third-party recipients
No return/destruction obligation on termination
Unilateral obligations only on receiving party

intellectualProperty.name: Intellectual Property Clause
intellectualProperty.description: A clause governing ownership, licensing, and use of intellectual property created or used under the contract
standardFeatures: Clear ownership of pre-existing IP retained by each party
License grant for use of background IP as needed
Clear ownership of newly created IP (work-for-hire or joint)
IP indemnity for infringement claims
Moral rights waiver where applicable
Reasonable license to use deliverables post-termination
redFlags: Automatic assignment of all IP created during engagement
No carve-out for pre-existing IP
Overly broad IP assignment covering improvements to background IP
No license grant for use of necessary background IP
Assignment of IP without consideration
Perpetual, irrevocable license with no scope limitations

disputeResolution.name: Dispute Resolution Clause
disputeResolution.description: A clause specifying the process and forum for resolving disputes between parties
standardFeatures: Tiered escalation: negotiation → mediation → arbitration/litigation
Clear choice of law and jurisdiction
Agreed arbitration institution and rules (if arbitration)
Seat and language of arbitration
Interim/emergency relief carve-out
Cost allocation provisions
redFlags: Exclusive jurisdiction in a party's home jurisdiction only
No carve-out for emergency injunctive relief
Mandatory arbitration waiving jury trial without clear disclosure
No clear governing law
Dispute resolution process heavily favoring one party
Class action waiver without equivalent benefit

forceMAjeure.name: Force Majeure Clause
forceMAjeure.description: A clause excusing a party from performance due to extraordinary events beyond its control
standardFeatures: Definition limited to genuinely unforeseeable events
Obligation to give prompt notice of force majeure event
Obligation to mitigate impact and resume performance
Right to terminate if force majeure continues beyond set period
No relief for financial hardship alone
Payment obligations survive force majeure
redFlags: Overly broad definition covering foreseeable events
No obligation to give notice
No obligation to mitigate
Relief from payment obligations during force majeure
No termination right if force majeure is prolonged
Including economic downturns or market changes as force majeure

generalRedFlags: Asymmetric obligations heavily favouring one party
No limitation on damages or liability
Automatic renewal with no notice mechanism
Waiver of statutory rights that cannot be waived

riskLevels.Low: Minor deviations from market standard. Acceptable with minor negotiation.
riskLevels.Moderate: Notable deviations that could create commercial exposure. Should be negotiated.
riskLevels.High: Significant one-sided terms or missing protections. Requires negotiation before execution.
